title: StcStory
description: A Save-the-Cat story — the dialect's root record per design/save-the-cat-sketch-01.md S4/S5. Aggregates beats, strands, and characters by id-string arrays (PFS7-X1 flat-with-id-refs topology; contrasts Aristotelian's tree-with-inline-$ref pattern per PFS6-X1). Declares an optional genre (S5, plain-string reference to a dialect-catalog StcGenre; resolution audit is PFS7 OQ4) and the canonical theme statement (S4, promoted to Story level so other tooling can reference it without parsing the Theme Stated beat content). Per save-the-cat-sketch-02 S12, archetype_assignments binds each genre archetype to a character or a prose note; the sub-record ships inline per PFS7-X2.

archetype_assignment.description: One binding between a genre archetype label and either a character id or a prose note per save-the-cat-sketch-02 S12. Binding-only record (no authorial id); ships inline per PFS7-X2. The S13 exactly-one-of-character_id-or-note constraint stays at the verifier (empty-vs-non-empty-string is a runtime-semantic check JSON Schema doesn't express cleanly); the schema admits both fields as optional.

archetype.description: The genre archetype label this assignment binds. Must match a label in the declared StcGenre's archetypes for the S13 archetype-coverage check to pass; schema admits any non-empty string (coverage is a verifier concern). PFS7-ST6.

character_id.description: Optional plain-string reference to a StcCharacter id when the archetype binds to a character (e.g., Whydunit's 'the detective' → Poirot). Resolution audit is PFS7 OQ4. PFS7-ST6.

note.description: Optional prose note when the archetype binds to non-character content (e.g., Rites of Passage's 'the life problem' → 'Macbeth's ambition + the witches' prophecy ...'). Python default empty string; schema admits unconditionally. PFS7-ST6.

id.description: Stable identifier for this story (e.g., 'stc_macbeth', 'stc_ackroyd'). PFS7-ST2.

title.description: Title of the work (e.g., 'Macbeth', 'The Murder of Roger Ackroyd'). PFS7-ST2.

theme_statement.description: Optional theme claim (the content the Theme Stated beat dramatizes, promoted to Story level per S4). Python default empty string; schema admits unconditionally. Verifier surfaces emptiness as NOTED 'theme_statement_empty'. PFS7-ST3.

stc_genre_id.description: Optional plain-string reference to a dialect-catalog StcGenre (one of Snyder's ten genres per S5). Resolution to GENRE_BY_ID is the S6 verifier's job; schema treats as plain string per PFS7-X3 (parallel to PFS6-X2 posture). PFS7-ST4.

beat_ids.description: Optional array of plain-string references to sibling StcBeat records per PFS7-X1 (flat-with-id-refs topology). Empty array legal — admits progressive authoring. Full encoding has 15 ids (one per canonical slot per S1); S6 verifier's beat-slot-coverage check enforces. PFS7-ST5.

strand_ids.description: Optional array of plain-string references to sibling StcStrand records per PFS7-X1. Empty array legal. Conventional full encoding has 2 (one A, one B per S3); S6 verifier's strand-kind check surfaces deviations as NOTED. PFS7-ST5.

character_ids.description: Optional array of plain-string references to sibling StcCharacter records per PFS7-X1 + save-the-cat-sketch-02 S11. Empty array legal — encodings pre-dating sketch-02 continue to verify without wiring characters. When set, S13 verifier checks (character-id resolution, one-protagonist advisory, archetype coverage) activate. PFS7-ST5.

archetype_assignments.description: Optional array of archetype-assignment sub-records per save-the-cat-sketch-02 S12. Inline $defs per PFS7-X2 (StcArchetypeAssignment has no authorial id and appears only on StcStory). Python default empty tuple → empty array. When non-empty without stc_genre_id set, S13 surfaces 'archetype_assignments_without_genre'. PFS7-ST6.

co_presence_requirements.description: S14-SE2 (save-the-cat-sketch-03 / DCS1 side 1). Optional hard co-presence constraints flowing through compilation stages 1–3. Empty array is the Python default; pre-sketch-03 encodings carry no requirements. S16.2 enforces structural integrity (>= 2 character refs; characters reachable from story.character_ids ∩ characters collection; slot in 1..15; min_count >= 1) plus participation against current authoring. Cross-file $ref to co_presence_requirement.json — follows the Aristotelian mythos.json precedent PFS-N13 and departs from PFS7-X1's flat-with-id-refs for sub-record arrays that have a dedicated schema. Field name deliberately shared with Aristotelian's `co_presence_requirements` per sketch-03's DOQ2 judgment. PFS-15-11.

strand_convergence_requirements.description: S14-SE3 (save-the-cat-sketch-03 / DCS1 side 1). Optional hard strand-convergence constraints flowing through compilation stages 1–3. Empty array is the Python default. S16.3 enforces structural integrity (>= 2 strand refs; strands reachable from story.strand_ids ∩ strands collection; slot in 1..15) plus advancement against current authoring. Dialect-specific: strands are an STC primitive with no Aristotelian analog. Cross-file $ref to strand_convergence_requirement.json. PFS-15-12.

tonal_register.description: S15-SP1 (save-the-cat-sketch-03 / DCS1 side 2). Soft tonal preference for stage-4 ranker scoring. Empty string (default) is neutral. Canonical-plus-open: published canonical vocabulary {tragic, comedic, thriller, elevated, grounded, hybrid}; non-canonical strings are admissible. Per DCS3 no verifier coverage. Field name deliberately shared with Aristotelian's `tonal_register` (A16-SP1 / PFS-N15) per sketch-03's DOQ2 judgment — the two vocabularies overlap on `tragic` and diverge elsewhere; the shared field name is the cross-dialect cognitive-map signal. PFS-15-13.

genre_adherence_preference.description: S15-SP2 (save-the-cat-sketch-03 / DCS1 side 2). Soft ranker-objective preference about how tightly the encoding hews to the declared genre's archetypes. Empty string (default) is neutral. Canonical vocabulary: {strict, loose, inventive, genre-bending}; non-canonical strings admissible. Per DCS3 no verifier coverage. Dialect-specific (Save the Cat's `Genre` concept; no Aristotelian analog). Open-string posture matches Aristotelian binding_distance_preference (PFS-N16) and STC's other S15 field. PFS-15-14.

authored_by.description: Optional provenance string. Python default 'author'. PFS7-ST.
